sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.41 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$F$be a free group of rank 2. a) It is known that$scl(g)=0$only for$g=1$, and$scl(g) 1/2$for all$1 g [F,F]$. Is$1/2$an isolated value? b) Are there any intervals$J$in${ R}$such that the set of values of$scl$on$[F,F]$is dense in$J$? c) Is there some$T { R}$such that every rational number${} T$is a value of$scl(g)$for$g [F,F]$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
